etymology.description: Originally 𫠤, a pictogram: a person 人 with a headdress -> stylized as 夭 -> 攴 added for authority -> transformed to 𢾍 -> person under headdress stylized as 方
etymology.interpretation: The character 敖(敖) (áo, "sound of loud talking") evolved through a series of pictographic and phono-semantic transformations, where the headdress and authority connotations were emphasized with the addition of 攴(攴) (pū, "rap") to the stylized person form, conveying an authoritative nature associated with loud talking.
